title: 22 Short Films About Springfield

jokes.text: I try not to. It makes it harder to spit on 'em.
jokes.setup: Bart asks if Milhouse thinks about people in cars
jokes.punchline: Milhouse admits he avoids empathy because it interferes with his petty cruelty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Milhouse's casual cruelty delivered with perfect child logic. Dark but perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Do you ever think about people in cars?

dialogue.speaker: Milhouse
dialogue.line: No, I try to avoid empathy. It interferes with my petty cruelty.

jokes.text: Milhouse zones out completely when he sees a convertible
jokes.setup: Bart is giving a philosophical speech about Springfield's stories
jokes.punchline: Milhouse is completely distracted by a passing convertible
jokes.explanation: Perfect kid behavior - completely missing the point for something shiny. Good character work.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Every town has stories to tell, Springfield has more than most. Tales of triumph and defeat, of love and loss, of—

dialogue.speaker: Milhouse
dialogue.line: Whoa, check out that convertible!

jokes.text: for the next five minutes I'm going to party like it's on sale for 19.99
jokes.setup: Apu agrees to party briefly
jokes.punchline: He converts 'party like it's 1999' into retail pricing terms
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that's perfectly Apu - takes a pop culture reference and makes it retail-specific. Highly quotable and character-driven.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I will party, but only briefly.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I will party like it's 1999... marked down from 1998!

jokes.text: Quick, quick. No time to cook them. They will plump in my stomach.
jokes.setup: Apu is rushing to eat at the party
jokes.punchline: He's eating raw hot dogs because he's too impatient to cook them
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with Apu's characteristic efficiency taken to absurd extremes.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I cannot wait for these to cook! I will eat them raw!

jokes.text: No ring, I see. So you are only arranged to be married.
jokes.setup: Apu hits on a woman at the party
jokes.punchline: He assumes no ring means she's only arranged to be married (not actually married yet)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cultural comedy - Apu's pick-up line reflects his cultural background and assumptions about marriage arrangements.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I couldn't help but notice you have no wedding ring. So you are merely betrothed, yes? Not actually married yet?

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: No, I'm married. I just don't wear my ring.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Ah, so there is still hope!

jokes.text: Don't worry. I'll tell everybody you were untouchable.
jokes.setup: Woman leaves after Apu's advances
jokes.punchline: Apu promises to protect her reputation using a caste system reference
jokes.explanation: Sophisticated cultural humor - 'untouchable' works as both social propriety and caste reference. Perfect Apu character work.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Fear not! I shall protect your honor by claiming you are of the Brahmin caste, highest in all of India!

jokes.text: You took four minutes of my life, and I want them back.
jokes.setup: Apu returns from the party one minute before his five-minute limit
jokes.punchline: Snake demands compensation for the four minutes Apu was gone
jokes.explanation: Snake's petty criminality - he'll steal time itself. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Hey, man, you owe me four minutes of compensation!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: But Snake, I returned before my five-minute limit was up.

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Yeah, but you were only gone for one minute, man. That means four minutes of my time is unaccounted for. You gotta pay up!

jokes.text: Oh, I'd only waste 'em anyway.
jokes.setup: Snake demands four minutes of life back
jokes.punchline: Apu casually dismisses the value of those minutes
jokes.explanation: Apu's practical resignation - defuses the situation with self-deprecation.

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Hey! You owe me four minutes of my life back!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Four minutes? That is nothing. I have wasted more time than that deciding which Squishee flavor to recommend.

jokes.text: Maybe it's just shampoo. That washes right out.
jokes.setup: Lisa has gum in her hair
jokes.punchline: Marge hopefully suggests it might be something that washes out easily
jokes.explanation: Marge's optimistic denial - hoping for the best case scenario. Mild character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, maybe it's just bubble gum. Those usually wash out pretty easily.

jokes.text: if I remember my Heloise, the trick to getting out gum... is peanut butter.
jokes.setup: Marge remembers advice for removing gum
jokes.punchline: She recalls specific household hint authority and solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - references household hint columnist. Sets up the escalating home remedy disaster.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh, I remember now. Ann Landers said to use peanut butter.

jokes.text: I smell like a sandwich.
jokes.setup: Marge has put peanut butter and mayonnaise in Lisa's hair
jokes.punchline: Lisa realizes she now smells like food
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - the logical conclusion of putting sandwich ingredients in hair.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... I smell like a sandwich.

jokes.text: They cause me to, uh, die.
jokes.setup: Smithers explains his bee allergy
jokes.punchline: He understates the ultimate consequence with hesitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers - polite understatement of death. The hesitation and economy make it brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: I'm allergic to bees. The last time I was stung, my throat swelled up and I... I couldn't breathe for several minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: The doctor said if it happens again, I'll... well, I'll probably die.

jokes.text: Oh, 'tuddle' Sunday trousers.
jokes.setup: Burns needs to pedal the bicycle
jokes.punchline: He uses an absurdly archaic and nonsensical curse
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Burns - invents antiquated profanity that makes no sense but sounds period-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Blast! The contraption won't move! By the ancient gods of commerce and the dust of my forbears, I curse this infernal two-wheeled contraption and all who dare suggest I pedal it myself!

jokes.text: Stick your left hoof on that flange now!
jokes.setup: Burns gives Smithers pedaling instructions
jokes.punchline: He calls Smithers' foot a 'hoof' and the pedal a 'flange'
jokes.explanation: Burns' dehumanizing vocabulary and mechanical terminology for bicycle parts.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: No, no, Smithers! Push with the hoof!

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: It's a foot, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Well, push it on the flange!

jokes.text: Now pump those scrawny chicken legs, you stuporous funker!
jokes.setup: Burns continues instructing Smithers
jokes.punchline: Elaborate insults mixing anatomy and made-up words
jokes.explanation: Burns at his most verbally abusive - 'stuporous funker' is inventive profanity that sounds authentically old-timey.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: You're a spineless, lily-livered, knuckle-dragging Neanderthal, Smithers. Your brain is smaller than a walnip and twice as wrinkled. You couldn't pour water out of a boot if the instructions were on the heel. You're a festering carbuncle on the backside of humanity, a walking advertisement for birth control, and your face looks like it lost a fight with a rake made of hammers.

jokes.text: Holy smokes! You need booze.
jokes.setup: Smithers arrives at hospital with bee sting
jokes.punchline: Barney diagnoses alcohol as the medical solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney - his solution to everything is alcohol, delivered as serious medical advice.

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Bee sting? That calls for a drink!

jokes.text: But I cleaned them with my napkin.
jokes.setup: Accused of operating with restaurant utensils
jokes.punchline: Dr. Nick thinks wiping with a napkin makes it sterile
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Nick incompetence - thinks basic napkin cleaning makes surgery tools safe.

dialogue.speaker: Judge
dialogue.line: Dr. Nick, you're accused of operating with restaurant utensils.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: Your Honor, I object! I always wipe them clean with a napkin first.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: That makes them sterile!

jokes.text: I get here earlier when I drive in the car pool lane.
jokes.setup: Accused of misusing cadavers
jokes.punchline: He uses dead bodies as carpool passengers for traffic lanes
jokes.explanation: Hilariously dark and practical - Dr. Nick finds efficient use for corpses in traffic management.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: Well, I use the cadavers for the carpool lane! It's a win-win — I get to use the fast lane, and the corpses... well, they're not complaining!

jokes.text: You're going to give yourself skin failure.
jokes.setup: Patient describes anxiety symptoms
jokes.punchline: Dr. Nick invents a nonsensical medical condition
jokes.explanation: Dr. Nick's medical incompetence - 'skin failure' sounds medical but makes no sense.

dialogue.speaker: Patient
dialogue.line: Doctor, I've been having terrible anxiety. I can't sleep, my heart races, and I feel like something bad is going to happen.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: Anxiety, you say? I've seen this before. You've got a classic case of Hexenbrenner's Syndrome.

dialogue.speaker: Patient
dialogue.line: Hexenbrenner's Syndrome? I've never heard of that.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: Of course you haven't! I just made it up. But don't worry, I've got just the thing—a pill I invented this morning!

jokes.text: bonus eruptus... where the skeleton tries to leap out the mouth and escape the body
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick diagnoses the patient
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up medical condition with vivid, impossible description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake medical terminology with disturbing imagery. Classic Dr. Nick at his most creatively incompetent.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick Riviera
dialogue.line: Well, what we have here is a classic case of... uh... Boneitis.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick Riviera
dialogue.line: It's a terrible disease where the bones get all... soft... and the diseases just... come right through the skin and start eating all the organs and everything.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick Riviera
dialogue.line: Eh, if I don't do something, this man could die... or worse, become a vegetable. It'd take a miracle.

jokes.text: transdental electromicide... golf cart motor with a 1,000-volt capacimator
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick proposes treatment
jokes.punchline: Made-up procedure using golf cart parts with fake technical terms
jokes.explanation: Dr. Nick's pseudo-scientific treatment mixing dental terms with electrocution and golf carts.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: What we have here is a classic case of... uh... *shuffles through medical chart* ...Simpsonian Deltoid Syndrome. Now, I've developed a revolutionary treatment using reconditioned golf cart parts and a semi-functional defibrillator I won at a church raffle.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: We'll attach the steering column here for stabilization, run the battery cables through the central nervous system, and—this is the important part—we'll need to charge it every six hours or the whole thing just sort of... stops.

jokes.text: Give me a Van Heflin.
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick offers free nose jobs
jokes.punchline: Patient requests nose job named after obscure 1940s actor
jokes.explanation: Absurd reference to minor actor as if it's a standard nose type. Weird and specific.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick Riviera
dialogue.line: Welcome! I'm Dr. Nick Riviera. I'm offering free nose jobs today.

dialogue.speaker: Patient
dialogue.line: Really? I'd like a nose job.

dialogue.speaker: Patient
dialogue.line: Can you make it look like John Carradine's?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick Riviera
dialogue.line: Who?

jokes.text: You owe me $70 billion... No wait, that's for the Voyager spacecraft. Your tab's $14 billion.
jokes.setup: Moe calculated Barney's bar tab
jokes.punchline: Astronomical amount, then correction revealing NASA mix-up
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and misdirection - the correction somehow makes it worse while being more believable.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Let's see here... that'll be $5,000... no wait, $50,000... actually $500,000!

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Hold on, I'm reading the wrong paper. This is NASA's budget.

jokes.text: Well, that's halfway there.
jokes.setup: Barney offers $2,000 toward $14 billion tab
jokes.punchline: Moe treats it as significant progress
jokes.explanation: Moe's terrible math and optimism. The deadpan delivery sells the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Here's two grand toward my tab.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Two thousand dollars? Why, that's... *checks calculator* ...that's only fourteen billion short!

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: We're making progress, Barney!

jokes.text: I'm behind three inches of bulletproof glass. Do your worst.
jokes.setup: Snake threatens to blow off Barney's head
jokes.punchline: Moe feels completely safe behind barrier
jokes.explanation: Good setup for visual gag where glass presumably fails to protect

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: I'm gonna blow your head clean off!

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well, I got news for you, pal. You're gonna have to go through me first!

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Alright.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! I got a gun under this counter, and it's pointed right at ya!

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Moe, is that true?

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: You're darn right it's true! I feel completely safe behind this here counter!

jokes.text: Stay out of there! Oh! Good God, no!
jokes.setup: Snake threatens to rob Moe
jokes.punchline: Moe's horror at Snake entering his cash register area
jokes.explanation: Moe's protective instinct about his business. Physical comedy implied through dialogue.

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Alright, hand over all your money, man.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Oh no, not again! Snake, please, anything but the cash register!

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, that's the whole point, dude.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: No! My sacred register! That's where I keep my... well, my money, but still!

jokes.text: Good-bye student loan payments.
jokes.setup: Snake successfully robs Moe's
jokes.punchline: His motivation is debt relief, not criminal lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Good character moment making Snake relatable - he's just trying to pay off education debt

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Dude, I'm not robbing this place 'cause I'm a criminal. I got like thirty grand in student loans, man.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Student loans? You went to college?

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Yeah, dude. Two years of community college before I dropped out. Now Sallie Mae won't leave me alone.

jokes.text: Oh, egads! My roast is ruined. But what if... I were to purchase fast food and disguise it as my own cooking?
jokes.setup: Skinner's roast is burned
jokes.punchline: He considers elaborate deception rather than admitting failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner - his panic and tendency toward elaborate schemes. Sets up the entire 'Steamed Hams' sequence.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Well, I could tell everyone the roast is ruined, or... I could tell them it's a new recipe I'm trying: Cajun-style, charred on the outside, still raw on the inside.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Yes, that's it. I'll say the smokiness is intentional. Very trendy in New Orleans.

jokes.text: Delightfully devilish, Seymour.
jokes.setup: Skinner plans his deception
jokes.punchline: He congratulates himself with theatrical villainy
jokes.explanation: Skinner's self-awareness and dramatic flair. Became a classic meme line.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Excellent. Now that I've hidden the evidence, no one will ever know what I've done. Mwahahaha!

jokes.text: I was just stretching my calves on the windowsill. Isometric exercise. Care to join me?
jokes.setup: Chalmers catches Skinner at window
jokes.punchline: Absurd excuse for obviously sneaking out to get fast food
jokes.explanation: Brilliant improvisation under pressure. The specificity of 'isometric exercise' makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: Skinner! What are you doing at that window?

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Oh, Superintendent Chalmers! I was just... checking the structural integrity of the glass. For safety purposes. Very important work.

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: At 2 AM? With a bag from Krusty Burger?

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: This? This is a... specimen collection for the science department. Educational. The children need to understand fast food's impact on— well, on everything.

jokes.text: That isn't smoke. It's steam. Steam from the steamed clams we're having.
jokes.setup: Chalmers notices smoke from oven
jokes.punchline: Skinner improvises elaborate lie about steamed clams
jokes.explanation: Skinner's quick thinking creates the central lie. The repetition of 'steam/steamed' shows his nervous energy.

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: Seymour, why is there smoke coming out of your oven?

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Uh... that isn't smoke. It's steam. Steam from the steamed clams we're having for dinner.

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: Steamed clams?

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Yes. That's what I call hamburgers.

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: You call hamburgers steamed clams?

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Yes, it's a regional dialect.

jokes.text: I said steamed hams. That's what I call hamburgers... Yes. It's a regional dialect.
jokes.setup: Caught changing from clams to hamburgers
jokes.punchline: Invents regional dialect explanation for nonsensical term
jokes.explanation: The birth of 'steamed hams' - perfect escalation of Skinner's desperation. Became legendary meme material.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Well, it's an old family recipe. In Springfield, we call it a 'Krusty Burger'.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But isn't that just a hamburger?

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: No, no. It's a regional dialect. You see, it's pronounced 'Krusty,' not 'hamburger.'

jokes.text: Oh, not in Utica. No. It's an Albany expression.
jokes.setup: Chalmers is from Utica and hasn't heard the term
jokes.punchline: Skinner gets more specific with his fake regional dialect
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Skinner doubles down with geographic specificity that makes no sense.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Oh, you're not familiar with the term? It's a regional dialect of the upper midwest.

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: No, no, not really.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Well, I'm from Utica and I've never heard anyone use the phrase 'steamed hams.'

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Oh, not in Utica, no. It's an Albany expression.

jokes.text: Patented Skinner burgers. Old family recipe... For steamed hams.
jokes.setup: Chalmers notes similarity to Krusty Burger
jokes.punchline: Skinner claims family recipe while maintaining the steamed hams fiction
jokes.explanation: Skinner adds more layers to his lie while staying committed to the steamed hams concept.

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Chalmers
dialogue.line: You know, these steamed hams are quite similar to a Krusty Burger.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Oh no, no, no. This is a family recipe passed down for generations. It has nothing to do with Krusty Burger.

dialogue.speaker: Superintendent Chalmers
dialogue.line: Hmm, the patty seems identical.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Well, you know how it is. Great minds think alike. But I assure you, these are steamed hams, and this is purely coincidental.

jokes.text: Aurora borealis... at this time of year, at this time of day, in this part of the country, localized entirely within your kitchen?
jokes.setup: Skinner claims house fire is aurora borealis
jokes.punchline: Chalmers methodically points out how impossible this claim is
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdity meets logical breakdown. Chalmers' systematic destruction of Skinner's claim is comedy gold.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Aurora borealis? At this time of year? At this time of day? In this part of the country? Localized entirely within your kitchen?

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: May I see it?

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: Aurora borealis at this time of year... at this time of day in this part of the country... localized entirely within your kitchen?
jokes.setup: Skinner claims aurora borealis explains the fire
jokes.punchline: Chalmers methodically lists why this is impossible
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant escalation. Chalmers' methodical deconstruction became one of the most quoted Simpsons lines ever.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Aurora borealis? At this time of year? At this time of day? In this part of the country? Localized entirely within your kitchen?

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: May I see it?

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: Yes... May I see it? ...No.
jokes.setup: Chalmers asks to see the supposed aurora borealis
jokes.punchline: Skinner flatly refuses without explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - the blunt 'No' after all his elaborate lies. Peak comedy timing and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: Skinner, I'd like to see this aurora borealis you mentioned.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: Yes. - May I see it? - No.
jokes.setup: Chalmers asks to see the supposed aurora borealis
jokes.punchline: Skinner's flat refusal
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedy timing. The immediate 'No' is brilliantly deadpan and became highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: Seymour, I'd like to see the aurora borealis.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: you are an odd fellow... but I must say you steam a good ham.
jokes.setup: Chalmers has witnessed all of Skinner's lies
jokes.punchline: He acknowledges the absurdity but accepts the steamed hams premise
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to the sequence - Chalmers' polite acceptance despite obvious lies. Classic social awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: You know, Skinner, I've witnessed all of your lies, all of your deceptions. And yet here we are, discussing steamed hams.

dialogue.speaker: Chalmers
dialogue.line: The absurdity is not lost on me. But I accept it.

jokes.text: Come on, Maggie. Maybe if you just squeeze a little.
jokes.setup: Homer encourages baby stuck in vending machine
jokes.punchline: Treating it like she can help her own rescue
jokes.explanation: Good Homer parenting incompetence but fairly standard for his character

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Come on, sweetie. You gotta help daddy get you out of there.

dialogue.speaker: Maggie
dialogue.line: *sucking pacifier*

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Push on the glass! Use those chubby little hands!

jokes.text: The dog.
jokes.setup: Homer needs to get help for Maggie stuck in car
jokes.punchline: His eureka moment is to use the family dog
jokes.explanation: Homer's simple solution delivered with dramatic revelation. Economy works well.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait a minute. Santa's Little Helper can help me!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Here boy! Get Maggie out of the car!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh! He's just licking the window.

jokes.text: No! Bad dog! Bad dog!
jokes.setup: Homer sends dog to get help
jokes.punchline: Dog apparently runs the wrong direction or fails immediately
jokes.explanation: Typical pet comedy - the dog immediately fails its mission. Predictable but functional.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Go get help, boy!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Santa's Little Helper!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why is he running toward the woods? No, no, come back!

jokes.text: You know, I went to the McDonald's in, uh, Shelbyville on Friday night. / The Mc-what?
jokes.setup: Wiggum mentions McDonald's
jokes.punchline: Lou acts like McDonald's doesn't exist in Simpsons universe
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about fast food parody vs reality in the show's universe

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: I'm going to McDonald's for lunch.

dialogue.speaker: Lou
dialogue.line: McDonald's? What's that? Never heard of it.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: You know, the fast food place with the golden arches?

dialogue.speaker: Lou
dialogue.line: Sorry Chief, I don't know what you're talking about.

jokes.text: But they don't call it a Krusty Burger with cheese. / Get out. Well, what do they call it? / A Quarter Pounder with cheese.
jokes.setup: Comparison between McDonald's and Krusty Burger
jokes.punchline: Real vs fictional product names
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of Pulp Fiction's Royale with Cheese scene, adapted to Simpsons universe

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about the difference between McDonald's and Krusty Burger.

dialogue.speaker: Lou
dialogue.line: Yeah, Chief? What's that?

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Well, McDonald's is a real place with real names for their food. Krusty Burger just makes stuff up!

dialogue.speaker: Lou
dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Like a 'Ribwich' - I mean, come on! That's not even a real word!

jokes.text: A Quarter Pounder with cheese... Quarter Pounder with cheese? Well, I can picture the cheese, but...
jokes.setup: Learning about McDonald's menu terminology
jokes.punchline: Wiggum is confused by weight-based naming but focused on cheese visualization
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's simple mind focuses on the concrete detail he can understand while missing the bigger point.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: A quarter pounder? What's that mean? A quarter of what?

dialogue.speaker: Other Character
dialogue.line: A quarter pound of beef.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Ohhh, a quarter pound! So what's a Whopper?

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Wait, I'm more interested in whether it has cheese. Is there cheese on it?

jokes.text: They call 'em shakes... 'Shakes.' You don't know what you're gettin'.
jokes.setup: Discussion of McDonald's beverage terminology
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's suspicion of simplified naming conventions
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's paranoid interpretation of marketing simplification. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Lou
dialogue.line: Hey Chief, why does McDonald's call it a 'Large' instead of a 'Big Gulp'?

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Well Lou, that's because they're trying to confuse us honest Americans. Next thing you know, they'll be calling a donut a 'fried dough circle.'

dialogue.speaker: Lou
dialogue.line: But Chief, that's basically what it is...

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Exactly! Which is why we can't trust 'em.

jokes.text: Ay. Que día miserable a trabajo.
jokes.setup: Bumblebee Man's day of mishaps begins
jokes.punchline: Spanish complaint about terrible work day
jokes.explanation: Standard Bumblebee Man setup but establishes character and situation well

dialogue.speaker: Bumblebee Man
dialogue.line: ¡Ay, qué día terrible! Mi traje se rompió, perdí mi guión, y nadie me reconoció sin el disfraz. ¡Esto es lo peor que me ha pasado en toda mi carrera!

jokes.text: Ay.!: Naranjas en la cabeza.!
jokes.setup: More mishaps befall Bumblebee Man
jokes.punchline: Oranges hitting his head
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy with Spanish exclamation

dialogue.speaker: Bumblebee Man
dialogue.line: Why do oranges keep hitting my head?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Because you're standing under an orange tree, you idiot!

jokes.text: Better get the old snow shovel back from Homer, huh?
jokes.setup: Dog defecates on Ned's lawn
jokes.punchline: Ned's practical concern about retrieving his borrowed snow shovel
jokes.explanation: Ned's neighborly practicality - even during embarrassing moments he thinks about borrowed property.

dialogue.speaker: Ned Flanders
dialogue.line: Well, I'd love to help clean that up, but I'm afraid I can't locate my snow shovel. I lent it to the Leftorium, and... well, let's just say retrieving it might require more intestinal fortitude than I currently possess!

jokes.text: I seem to have mashed more hair into it.
jokes.setup: Selma tries ice cube and hammer method
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of making the problem worse
jokes.explanation: Selma's blunt honesty about her failure. Good understatement of disaster.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: I tried the ice cube and hammer method.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: I think I made it worse.

jokes.text: Chewing gum's got to be chewed out.
jokes.setup: Various townspeople offer gum removal advice
jokes.punchline: Otto's stoner logic about gum needing to be chewed out
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto - his stoner logic that gum problems require gum solutions.

dialogue.speaker: Otto
dialogue.line: Dude, you gotta chew it out. That's the only way. Gum's gotta be chewed out by gum, man.

jokes.text: All right! Fangoria will give me 25 bucks for this shot.
jokes.setup: Seeing Lisa's hair disaster
jokes.punchline: Sees commercial opportunity in horror magazine photo submission
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy - monetizing others' misfortune through niche magazine submissions.

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: Worst. Hair. Ever. But wait... this could be a goldmine for my 'Profiles in Nerdery' submission to Horror Monthly!

jokes.text: Don't try to dig gum out with a bone. It just makes things worse.
jokes.setup: Dog offering advice on gum removal
jokes.punchline: Specific experience-based advice from the family dog
jokes.explanation: Absurd that the dog speaks, but his advice is surprisingly practical and specific.

dialogue.speaker: Santa's Little Helper
dialogue.line: You got gum stuck in your fur? Yeah, I've been there. Peanut butter works great. Or you could just let it sit there for three weeks like I did.

jokes.text: Leave it in as evidence. Bazooka Joe's got deep pockets.
jokes.setup: Hutz offers legal advice about the gum
jokes.punchline: Suggests lawsuit against gum company for having money
jokes.explanation: Classic Hutz - sees every problem as a potential lawsuit against whoever has money.

dialogue.speaker: Lionel Hutz
dialogue.line: Now, I'm no expert, but I believe the gum company has a lot of money. So I suggest we sue them.

jokes.text: And scuff up the topless dancin' runway? Nah.
jokes.setup: Cletus suggests shoes for her job interview
jokes.punchline: Reveals her job interview is for exotic dancing and prioritizes equipment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about rural poverty and limited job options, delivered with professional pride.

dialogue.speaker: Cletus
dialogue.line: Well now, Brandine, for that job interview, you're gonna need some sensible shoes.

dialogue.speaker: Brandine
dialogue.line: It's for exotic dancing, Cletus.

dialogue.speaker: Brandine
dialogue.line: I'm more worried about a pole.

jokes.text: Hey, Ma! Get off the dang roof!
jokes.setup: Cletus thinks of calling his mother
jokes.punchline: His mother is apparently on the roof of their house
jokes.explanation: Absurd rural family situation - casual treatment of dangerous behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Cletus
dialogue.line: I should call my mother.

dialogue.speaker: Cletus
dialogue.line: Oh, she's on the roof again.

jokes.text: That is a rare photo of Sean Connery signed by Roger Moore. It is worth $150.
jokes.setup: Kid asks what he can buy for 75 cents
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy describes worthless celebrity photo with inflated value
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy - overvaluing worthless collectibles with specific, meaningless details.

dialogue.speaker: Kid
dialogue.line: What can I buy for 75 cents?

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: Ah, seventy-five cents. Well, I have an autographed photo of Burt Reynolds. Now, normally this would be worth at least thirty dollars, but it has a small water stain, so I'm asking seventy-five cents.

dialogue.speaker: Kid
dialogue.line: That's still too much.

jokes.text: A child has already solved the Jumble using crayons. The answer is 'fries.'
jokes.setup: Offering 75-cent comic book option
jokes.punchline: Honestly describes the comic's pre-solved puzzle and simple answer
jokes.explanation: Comic Book Guy's brutal honesty about damaged merchandise while maintaining sales pitch.

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: Well, the crossword puzzle is already filled in, and someone's already circled the answer.

jokes.text: You keep squirming, there's going to be a little bald girl with no lollipop.
jokes.setup: Lisa is fidgeting during haircut
jokes.punchline: Hairdresser threatens both baldness and candy denial
jokes.explanation: Escalating threat that combines physical consequence with petty punishment. Good economy.

dialogue.speaker: Hairdresser
dialogue.line: Hold still, sweetie. If you keep moving around like that, you're gonna end up bald.

dialogue.speaker: Hairdresser
dialogue.line: And no lollipop for you!

jokes.text: This was the largest auto that I could afford. Should I therefore be made the subject of fun?
jokes.setup: Confronting Nelson about mocking his small car
jokes.punchline: Logical argument about economic constraints versus ridicule
jokes.explanation: Reasonable question that exposes the cruelty of mocking people's limitations. Good moral reversal.

dialogue.speaker: Tall Man
dialogue.line: Hey, why are you making fun of my car? I work hard for my money.

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: Ha-ha! Your car sucks!

dialogue.speaker: Tall Man
dialogue.line: Look, I'm on a budget. Not everyone can afford a fancy vehicle.

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: That's not my problem, dork!

jokes.text: Wave to the people. Blow them kisses.
jokes.setup: Tall Man forces Nelson to parade
jokes.punchline: Adds humiliating gestures to the public shaming
jokes.explanation: Escalates the humiliation with specific theatrical elements. Good revenge completion.

dialogue.speaker: Tall Man
dialogue.line: Nelson, you're going to parade through town so everyone can see what a bully you are.

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: No way, man!

dialogue.speaker: Tall Man
dialogue.line: And you're going to do these humiliating gestures while you walk.

dialogue.speaker: Tall Man
dialogue.line: Ha-ha!

jokes.text: Wave to the people. Blow them kisses.
jokes.setup: Tall man forces Nelson to be publicly humiliated
jokes.punchline: Making him perform for the crowd
jokes.explanation: Good comeuppance sequence with poetic justice for the bully

dialogue.speaker: Tall Man
dialogue.line: You're going to perform for the crowd.

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: Ha-ha!

jokes.text: There was trouble at the lab with the running and the exploding and the crying.
jokes.setup: Frink explains why he's late
jokes.punchline: Vague but alarming description of lab disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frink - casual reference to scientific disasters with childlike description.

dialogue.speaker: Professor Frink
dialogue.line: I'm sorry I'm late! There was a small incident at the lab.

dialogue.speaker: Professor Frink
dialogue.line: Nothing to worry about. Well, the containment field may have ruptured, and there might be some sort of radioactive cloud spreading toward the city, but other than that, everything's fine!

jokes.text: Professor Frink Professor Frink / He'll make you laugh He'll make you think / He likes to run and then the thing with the / Person
jokes.setup: Frink offers to tell story with theme music
jokes.punchline: His theme song breaks down mid-verse into incoherence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Frink moment - starts confidently but his scientific mind can't handle creative expression.

dialogue.speaker: Professor Frink
dialogue.line: Ah, yes, let me regale you with a tale accompanied by my theme music!

dialogue.speaker: Professor Frink
dialogue.line: Professor Frink, he's got a plan, he's working in his lab again, with a crazy contraption that will make you say 'What the—'

dialogue.speaker: Professor Frink
dialogue.line: Hoy-hoy-hoy, zippity-dop, floob-floob-a-diddly-doop, schlibby-flibby-flub, grrr-schnitzel-voodle-doodle-dap!

tmdb_overview: Bart and Milhouse wonder if anything interesting happens to the citizens of Springfield, which leads to a series of interconnected vignettes.
insight: Twenty-two vignettes sustain 87-point quality through relentless character escalation and absurdist premises.
